verses.text: He teaches from Simon's boat as the crowd presses to hear. Pressing throngs. The word draws the eager multitude.
He teaches from Simon's boat as the crowd presses to hear. Pressing throngs. The word draws the eager multitude.
He teaches from Simon's boat as the crowd presses to hear. Pressing throngs. The word draws the eager multitude.
'Launch out... let down your nets'; we toiled all night, 'nevertheless at thy word'; they take a great catch. Obedient faith. Trusting His word, they take what their labour could not.
'Launch out... let down your nets'; we toiled all night, 'nevertheless at thy word'; they take a great catch. Obedient faith. Trusting His word, they take what their labour could not.
'Launch out... let down your nets'; we toiled all night, 'nevertheless at thy word'; they take a great catch. Obedient faith. Trusting His word, they take what their labour could not.
'Launch out... let down your nets'; we toiled all night, 'nevertheless at thy word'; they take a great catch. Obedient faith. Trusting His word, they take what their labour could not.
Peter falls down, 'Depart... I am a sinful man'; fear not, 'thou shalt catch men'; they forsake all. Holy fear and call. The presence of Christ both humbles and commissions.
Peter falls down, 'Depart... I am a sinful man'; fear not, 'thou shalt catch men'; they forsake all. Holy fear and call. The presence of Christ both humbles and commissions.

A leper says, 'If thou wilt'; Jesus touches him, 'I will; be thou clean.' Willing touch. He cleanses the outcast none else would touch.
A leper says, 'If thou wilt'; Jesus touches him, 'I will; be thou clean.' Willing touch. He cleanses the outcast none else would touch.
A leper says, 'If thou wilt'; Jesus touches him, 'I will; be thou clean.' Willing touch. He cleanses the outcast none else would touch.
His fame spreads, great crowds come, but He withdraws to the wilderness to pray. Prayer amid pressure. The busier He is, the more He seeks the Father.
His fame spreads, great crowds come, but He withdraws to the wilderness to pray. Prayer amid pressure. The busier He is, the more He seeks the Father.
A palsied man is let down; seeing their faith, 'Man, thy sins are forgiven thee.' Deepest need first. He pardons the soul before He heals the body.
A palsied man is let down; seeing their faith, 'Man, thy sins are forgiven thee.' Deepest need first. He pardons the soul before He heals the body.
A palsied man is let down; seeing their faith, 'Man, thy sins are forgiven thee.' Deepest need first. He pardons the soul before He heals the body.

Who can forgive but God? 'that ye may know the Son of man hath power,' Arise; he rises. Divine claim proved. The cure confirms the power to forgive.
Who can forgive but God? 'that ye may know the Son of man hath power,' Arise; he rises. Divine claim proved. The cure confirms the power to forgive.

Who can forgive but God? 'that ye may know the Son of man hath power,' Arise; he rises. Divine claim proved. The cure confirms the power to forgive.
He departs glorifying God; all are amazed, 'We have seen strange things to day.' Wonder. The mercy of Christ astonishes the crowd.
He departs glorifying God; all are amazed, 'We have seen strange things to day.' Wonder. The mercy of Christ astonishes the crowd.
He calls Levi the publican, 'Follow me'; he leaves all and follows. Grace to a tax-man. The despised are not beyond His call.
He calls Levi the publican, 'Follow me'; he leaves all and follows. Grace to a tax-man. The despised are not beyond His call.
At Levi's feast the Pharisees murmur; 'I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.' The Physician. He seeks those who own their need.

Why fast not thy disciples? the bridechamber children cannot fast while the bridegroom is present. A time for joy. His presence turns mourning into gladness.
Why fast not thy disciples? the bridechamber children cannot fast while the bridegroom is present. A time for joy. His presence turns mourning into gladness.
Why fast not thy disciples? the bridechamber children cannot fast while the bridegroom is present. A time for joy. His presence turns mourning into gladness.
No man puts new cloth on old, nor new wine in old bottles. Something new. The gospel is no patch on the old order.
